panels.title: Ingest request rate
panels.description: runtime-api ingest HTTP request rate split by status code. A growing 4xx/5xx share means clients (the local collection daemon) cannot land events.

panels.title: Ingest latency (p50/p95/p99)
panels.description: runtime-api ingest HTTP handler latency percentiles. Sustained p95/p99 growth means ingest is slowing down under load, risking daemon-side request timeouts.

panels.title: runtime-db ledger max seq (growth rate)
panels.description: Monotonic runtime-db event ledger sequence and its growth rate. Rate dropping toward zero while ingest traffic keeps flowing means writes are not committing to runtime-db.

panels.title: CDC lag behind source
panels.description: Debezium connector lag behind runtime-db's WAL, in milliseconds. Crossing the 30s threshold means CDC has fallen far enough behind that Kafka (and everything downstream) is running on stale data.

panels.title: runtime-db replication slot unconsumed WAL
panels.description: Bytes of runtime-db WAL retained by the Debezium replication slot because the connector hasn't consumed them yet. A steady climb means the CDC connector is stalled or down and runtime-db risks disk exhaustion.

panels.title: Consumer group lag (ingest.events)
panels.description: Consumer group lag for ingest.events. A sustained climb means the projector cannot keep up with Redpanda.

panels.title: Broker E2E roundtrip latency (p95)
panels.description: p95 roundtrip latency of kminion's synthetic canary messages through Redpanda. Rising values mean the broker itself is slow, which drags down every real consumer group including the projector.

panels.title: E2E canary messages lost (1h)
panels.description: Count of kminion canary messages produced but never observed as consumed within the last hour. Any value above zero means Redpanda is dropping or failing to deliver messages.

panels.title: Applied seq gap (max_seq − applied)
panels.description: 0에 가까울수록 정상. lag이 0인데 갭이 줄지 않으면 유실 의심.

panels.title: Apply latency (received_at → commit), p95
panels.description: p95 time from an event's received_at to the projector committing it into tracer-db/OpenSearch. Rising latency here means the projector is slow to apply even while caught up on offsets, risking stale reads on tracer-api.

panels.title: Records processed (by outcome)
panels.description: Projector's per-second record processing rate broken down by outcome. A growing non-success share means the projector is failing to apply records, not just falling behind.

panels.title: tracer-db active connections
panels.description: Active backend connections held against the tracer database. A sustained climb toward the configured max_connections risks exhausting the pool and blocking tracer-api reads or projector writes.

panels.title: OpenSearch cluster status
panels.description: OpenSearch cluster health as reported by the exporter (the panel labels the healthy value as "up"). Anything else means tracer-api's OpenSearch-backed search endpoints are degraded or unavailable.

panels.title: Temporal activity schedule-to-start (p95)
panels.description: p95 time an AI job activity waits between being scheduled and picked up by ai-agent-worker, per task queue. Rising values mean the worker doesn't have enough capacity to keep up with scheduled work.

panels.title: Live vs. pending-export partitions
panels.description: pending>0가 오래 지속되면 cold:tier 잡이 스케줄대로 안 도는 것.

panels.title: MinIO cluster health
panels.description: MinIO cluster health status (the panel labels the healthy value as "healthy"). Any other value means the cold-tier archive target for pg_partman/DuckDB exports cannot durably accept writes.

panels.title: MinIO usable capacity free
panels.description: Usable free byte capacity remaining across the MinIO cluster that backs cold-tier exports. Capacity approaching zero blocks pg_partman's detached-partition export job and it will start failing.
